verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP synthase F1 beta subunit) is canonically a mitochondrial inner-membrane complex component facing the matrix. However, it has well-documented ectopic cell-surface expression ('ecto-F1-ATPase') on hepatocytes, endothelial cells, and notably tumor cells, where it acts as a receptor for apoA-I, angiostatin, and others. This cancer/tissue-associated ecto-pool has been pursued therapeutically, qualifying as contextual cell-state-induced surface display rather than no.
